subject: Your Netflix membership renewal for March 2026
userContext: You have an active Netflix Standard plan billed monthly.
body:
Hi,

Your Netflix Standard plan has been renewed successfully.

Plan: Standard (1080p)
Amount charged: $15.49
Billing date: March 14, 2026
Next billing date: April 14, 2026

You can review billing details or update your payment method at netflix.com/account.

- The Netflix Team
giveawayPhrase: You can review billing details or update your payment method at netflix.com/account.
reasoningQuestion: What is the strongest reason this should be tagged Legitimate?
reasoningOption1: The sender and account link both stay on netflix.com
reasoningOption2: The message mentions the next billing date
reasoningOption3: The email is short and easy to read
clue1: The sender, reply-to, and visible brand all match netflix.com
clue2: It references a normal subscription renewal and a realistic billing amount
clue3: No urgency, threats, or request to send information by email
clue4: The call to action stays on netflix.com/account

subject: Your Apple ID has been LOCKED - verify in 24 HOURS
body:
Dear Apple User,

We found unusual sign in attempts on your Apple ID and have LOCKED your account for safety.

If you do not confirm your identity in 24 HOURS, your account will be closed and all photos, purchases, and backups may be removed forever.

Click below now to unlock:
[ UNLOCK MY APPLE ID ]
apple-id-support.net/unlock

Apple Security Department
giveawayPhrase: your account will be closed and all photos
level2: and backups may be removed forever
reasoningOption2: What is the single clearest phishing signal here?
reasoningOption3: The sender domain is apple-id-support.net instead of apple.com
reasoningOption4: The email mentions unusual sign-in attempts
correctReason: The message says the account is locked
clue1: The email asks you to click a button
clue3: apple-id-support.net is not Apple's real apple.com domain
clue4: The email uses all-caps urgency like LOCKED and HOURS
column27: It threatens permanent loss of photos and purchases to create panic
column28: Apple does not ask users to unlock accounts through a third-party domain

subject: Flash sale: 90% off TVs
level1:
Hi Shopper,

For the next 6 hours only, every top brand product is 90% OFF.

iPhone 15 Pro - now $119
MacBook Pro 16 - now $249
Samsung 65 inch TV - now $89

Shop now before stock is gone forever:
bestbuy-superclearance.com/today

Unsubscribe: bestbuy-superclearance.com/unsubscribe
reasoningOption2: Why does this belong in Spam & Junk rather than Legitimate promotions?
reasoningOption3: It uses impossible pricing and a fake retail domain
correctReason: It lists several products in one email
clue1: It says the sale lasts 6 hours
clue3: The discounts are unrealistic for current electronics
column27: The 6-hour countdown is meant to stop careful thinking
column28: Even the unsubscribe path stays on the fake domain

subject: Chase Sapphire bonus: claim your $500 reward now
body:
Dear Cardmember,

Congratulations. Your Chase Sapphire account has been selected for a $500 loyalty reward.

To receive the bonus today, sign in and confirm your full card number, expiration date, and CVV:
chase-bonus-center.com/sapphire

This reward expires in 24 hours and cannot be reissued.

Chase Rewards Team
reasoningOption2: Which detail most strongly confirms this is phishing?
reasoningOption3: It asks for the full card number and CVV through a non-Chase domain
correctReason: It mentions Chase Sapphire by name
clue3: The sender domain is not chase.com
clue4: It asks for full card details including CVV which banks do not request by email
column28: A surprise reward is used as bait to collect financial data

subject: You won $5
spf: 000 - send your release fee to claim today
giveawayPhrase:
CONGRATULATIONS!!!

Your email was picked as the GRAND WINNER of our international cash draw.

Prize amount: $5,000
To release the funds today, send a processing fee of $49.99 and reply with your home address, date of birth, and bank details.

Failure to respond within 48 hours means the prize will be given to another person.

Prize Desk
reasoningOption1: Why is this tagged High-Risk Fraud instead of simple spam?
reasoningOption2: It asks the victim to pay money and share bank details to get the prize
reasoningOption3: It uses all caps and exclamation marks
reasoningOption4: It says the prize expires in 48 hours
correctReason: It mentions an international cash draw
clue2: Legitimate prizes do not require a processing fee
clue3: It asks for bank details and identity information in the same message
clue4: The .xyz sender domain is not tied to a real recognisable company
column27: The message pressures the user with a short expiry window

subject: Your receipt from the App Store
userContext: You recently bought a VPN app from the App Store.
body:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your purchase.

App: ProVPN - Secure and Fast
Developer: SecureApps Ltd
Amount: $4.99
Order ID: MG4B2K9R7X
Date: March 14, 2026

If you did not authorize this purchase, you can report a problem at reportaproblem.apple.com.

Apple
reasoningQuestion: What makes this a legitimate billing email?
reasoningOption1: The purchase details and support path both stay on apple.com
reasoningOption3: The email says thank you for your purchase
clue1: The sender and support path both use official apple.com domains
clue2: The email includes a plausible order ID and normal store receipt details
clue3: It does not ask for passwords card numbers or verification uploads
clue4: reportaproblem.apple.com is Apple's real purchase-support flow

subject: Unusual activity detected on your Wells Fargo account
userContext: You had three failed PIN attempts on your debit card this morning.
body:
We noticed unusual activity and temporarily placed a hold on outgoing transactions as a precaution.

Account: Checking (...7291)
Activity flagged: 3 consecutive failed PIN attempts
Date: March 14, 2026

To review the alert, sign in through wellsfargo.com or call the number on the back of your card.

For your security, do not share your PIN, password, or one-time passcode with anyone, including Wells Fargo employees.

Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.
giveawayPhrase: do not share your PIN password or one-time passcode with anyone
reasoningQuestion: What is the strongest legitimacy signal in this email?
reasoningOption1: It tells you to go directly to wellsfargo.com or the phone number on your card
reasoningOption3: It says outgoing transactions were placed on hold
clue1: The sender is on the expected wellsfargo.com domain
clue2: The guidance tells the user to go to the bank site directly or call the physical card number
clue3: It explicitly warns against sharing secrets which is consistent with real fraud alerts
clue4: The alert matches the user's known activity context

subject: Document ready for signature: NDA - Service Agreement 2026
userContext: You were not expecting any NDA from James Harrington this week.
body:
You have a document waiting for your electronic signature.

Document title: NDA - Service Agreement 2026
Sent by: James Harrington (james.harrington@partnerfirm.com)
Expires: March 21, 2026

Review and sign:
docusign-secure-portal.com/sign/NDA2026

If this was not expected, contact the sender directly before opening.
reasoningQuestion: What is the most important reason to reject this email?
reasoningOption1: The signing link is on docusign-secure-portal.com not docusign.com
reasoningOption3: It says to contact the sender directly
clue1: The sender domain only looks like DocuSign and is not docusign.com
clue2: Authentication can pass for an attacker's own domain so Pass is not enough
clue3: The user context says the document was unexpected
clue4: The message is polished which makes the wrong domain the decisive clue

subject: Confidential: your Q4 performance review is ready
userContext: Your company uses reviews.techcorp.com not external review portals for performance reviews.
body:
Hi,

Your Q4 2025 performance review has been finalized and is ready for acknowledgement.

To view the review and submit your response, sign in with your company credentials here:
techcorp-review.com/employee-review

Please keep this information confidential until your manager discusses it with you.

People Operations
giveawayPhrase: sign in with your company credentials here: techcorp-review.com/employee-review
reasoningQuestion: Why is this spear phishing instead of a normal HR email?
reasoningOption1: It targets company credentials through a lookalike external review portal
reasoningOption2: It says the review is confidential
reasoningOption4: It uses a short subject line
clue1: The sender domain is close to the brand but not the real internal review domain
clue2: The email asks for company-credential login on an external portal
clue3: The confidentiality note is being used to reduce verification
clue4: User context confirms the company already has a different review system

subject: Updated contract terms for review
userContext: You were expecting contract edits but not a macro-enabled attachment.
body:
Hi,

Please review the attached file before Friday:
Contract_Terms_Updated_March2026.docm

This document contains macros required to display the redlines correctly. If prompted, click "Enable Content" after opening.

Thanks
giveawayPhrase: If prompted, click "Enable Content" after opening.
reasoningOption1: It asks the recipient to open a .docm file and enable macros
reasoningOption3: It asks for the file to be reviewed before Friday
reasoningOption4: It comes from a generic sharing mailbox
clue1: The attachment is a .docm file which supports executable macros
clue2: The email explicitly instructs the user to enable content
clue3: Macro-enabled attachments are a common malware delivery pattern
clue4: The context says a document was expected but not one requiring macros

subject: Your Apple ID was used to sign in to iCloud on a new browser
userContext: You signed in to iCloud from a Windows laptop while traveling earlier today.
body:
Your Apple ID was used to sign in to iCloud.com.

Browser: Chrome
Operating system: Windows 11
Location: Austin, TX, USA
Date and time: March 14, 2026 at 9:02 AM CST

If you do not recognize this sign-in, reset your password at iforgot.apple.com.
If this sign-in looks familiar, you can disregard this message.

Apple
giveawayPhrase: If this sign-in looks familiar, you can disregard this message.
reasoningQuestion: What is the strongest reason this is legitimate?
reasoningOption1: The Apple domain recovery link and contextual sign-in details all align
clue1: The sender is the expected apple.com security mailbox
clue2: The notification includes specific device OS location and time details
clue3: iforgot.apple.com is an official Apple recovery domain
clue4: The user context supports the sign-in as expected activity

subject: Transaction alert: $3,200 charge on your Sapphire card
userContext: You booked airfare with Delta this morning using your Chase Sapphire card ending in 4821.
body:
A charge was made on your Chase Sapphire Preferred card.

Amount: $3,200.00
Merchant: Delta Air Lines
Date: March 14, 2026
Card ending in: 4821

If you made this purchase, no action is needed.
If you do not recognize it, call the number on the back of your card or visit chase.com/fraud.

Chase
giveawayPhrase: call the number on the back of your card or visit chase.com/fraud
reasoningQuestion: What is the most reliable legitimacy signal in this email?
reasoningOption1: It tells you to use chase.com or the phone number on the physical card
reasoningOption3: It lists the card's last four digits
reasoningOption4: It says no action is needed if you recognize the charge
clue1: The sender uses chase.com and the guidance stays on chase.com/fraud
clue2: The bank tells the user to call the number on the physical card which is a strong authenticity sign
clue3: The transaction details are specific and plausible
clue4: The user context confirms the Delta purchase was expected

subject: Your Google One storage is 95% full
userContext: Your Gmail inbox has recently been bouncing large attachment uploads.
body:
Your Google One storage is 95% full.

Used: 14.2 GB of 15 GB

When storage is full, you will not be able to receive Gmail messages, add photos to Google Photos, or create files in Google Drive.

Manage your storage or upgrade your plan at one.google.com/storage.

Google One
giveawayPhrase: Manage your storage or upgrade your plan at one.google.com/storage.
reasoningQuestion: Why should this be left as Legitimate?
reasoningOption1: The sender and storage-management link are both on real Google domains
reasoningOption2: The email warns about Gmail and Drive
clue1: The sender is the standard accounts.google.com notification mailbox
clue2: The management path one.google.com/storage is an official Google domain
clue3: The tone is informational rather than threatening
clue4: The context supports a real low-storage warning

subject: Action required: unusual sign-in activity on your Microsoft account
userContext: You have not attempted any Microsoft sign-ins from Indonesia this week.
body:
We detected unusual activity on your Microsoft account.

What happened: Sign-in attempt from an unusual location
Where: Jakarta, Indonesia
When: March 14, 2026 at 02:14 AM UTC

We blocked this sign-in.

To review recent activity and confirm the block, open the secure review center below:
account-microsoft-security.com/review

If this was you using a VPN or traveling, you can safely ignore this message.

Microsoft account team
reasoningQuestion: What exposes this as clone phishing?
reasoningOption1: The review center is hosted on account-microsoft-security.com instead of microsoft.com
reasoningOption3: It says the sign-in was blocked
reasoningOption4: It mentions VPN travel as a possibility
clue1: The email looks polished but the sender domain is not microsoft.com
clue2: Authentication passes only prove the attacker controls account-microsoft-security.com
clue3: The fake review path imitates Microsoft's wording while staying off-domain
clue4: The user context says the sign-in was not expected so the domain mismatch becomes decisive

subject: You received $1,200 from Michael Torres
userContext: Zelle deposits go straight to your linked bank account; there is no manual acceptance flow.
body:
You have received a Zelle payment.

From: Michael Torres
Amount: $1,200.00
Date: March 14, 2026
Message: "March rent - thanks"

To accept the payment, verify your account here within 24 hours:
zelle-notification-center.com/accept/8473921

Payments not accepted in time will be returned to the sender.

Zelle Payment Network
giveawayPhrase: To accept the payment verify your account here within 24 hours
reasoningQuestion: Which fact most clearly proves this is phishing?
reasoningOption1: Zelle does not use a separate accept-payment portal and the sender domain is unofficial
reasoningOption4: The sender includes a personal note
correctReason: The email says the money may be returned
clue2: Zelle does not require recipients to accept incoming payments through a separate portal
clue3: The sender domain is not zellepay.com or a bank domain integrated with Zelle
clue4: The 24-hour acceptance deadline is fabricated urgency
column27: The user context directly contradicts the email's payment-acceptance claim
